manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: engine, ambulance, rome, figure, iron, cook, pole, crash, draft.
Your opponent's team words are: spine, green, novel, chocolate, hole, boom, mexico, disease.
Distractor words are: england, poison, cell, embassy, track, revolution, plate, bugle.
The kill words are: .

boom, cook, cell, hole, bugle, rome, mexico, ambulance, chocolate, draft, plate, disease, embassy, pole, england, revolution, figure, track, poison, engine, spine, crash, novel, green, iron
GUESS: draft

The word draft was guessed correctly. Your turn ended there.
Now provide another comma-separated list of at least 1 and up to 8 words from the following list of words that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'wreck'. Remember to start your answer with 'GUESS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the list compared to previous requests.

boom, cook, cell, hole, bugle, rome, mexico, ambulance, chocolate, plate, disease, embassy, pole, england, revolution, figure, track, poison, engine, spine, crash, novel, iron
GUESS: crash, ambulance, engine, pole, track, disease, poison

The words crash, ambulance, engine, pole were guessed correctly. The word track was guessed but is an innocent word. Your turn ended there.
Now provide another comma-separated list of at least 1 and up to 4 words from the following list of words that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'cast'. Remember to start your answer with 'GUESS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the list compared to previous requests.

boom, cook, cell, hole, bugle, rome, mexico, chocolate, plate, disease, embassy, england, revolution, figure, poison, novel, iron
GUESS: iron, figure, plate, novel

The words iron, figure were guessed correctly. The word plate was guessed but is an innocent word. Your turn ended there.
Now provide another comma-separated list of at least 1 and up to 2 words from the following list of words that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'pasta'. Remember to start your answer with 'GUESS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the list compared to previous requests.

boom, cook, cell, hole, bugle, rome, mexico, chocolate, embassy, england, revolution, poison, novel

message.reasoning:
The user is asking me to guess words related to 'pasta' from a given list. Let me analyze the list:

Available words: boom, cook, cell, hole, bugle, rome, mexico, chocolate, embassy, england, revolution, poison, novel

Words that relate to 'pasta':
- cook: pasta is cooked, so this is a strong association
- rome: pasta is strongly associated with Italy/Rome
- mexico: less directly related than rome, but could be associated through cooking
- england: not particularly associated with pasta

The strongest associations would be:
1. cook (you cook pasta) - very direct
2. rome (Italy/Rome is famous for pasta) - very direct

I can only provide up to 2 words. I should choose the most closely related ones. "Cook" is the most direct association since pasta is a food that is cooked. "Rome" is also very strong due to the Italian connection.

Let me think about this more carefully. The pattern from previous rounds suggests we want the most obvious connections. For prototype → draft, it was about a preliminary version. For wreck → crash/ambulance/engine/pole, these are all things related to accidents. For cast → iron/figure/plate/novel, these relate to different meanings of "cast" (cast iron, cast of characters/figure, plaster cast/plate, novel cast).

So for pasta, "cook" is the most direct action associated with pasta. "Rome" is about the origin/culture. Both are strong. Since I can pick up to 2, I'll pick both.

GUESS: cook, rome
